description_long: This object is a stylized flower with five large, translucent lavender petals radiating outward from a central yellow-green core. The petals are broad, slightly curved, and exhibit delicate vein-like texturing, giving them a glass-like appearance. The center of the flower features a cluster of spherical, yellow-green structures resembling stamens, which extend outward on a short stalk. The overall form is organic and elegant, with the petals overlapping slightly and creating a sense of depth and layering. No stem or leaves are visible, and the flower appears to be a single, isolated bloom. The coloration is primarily a soft purple with contrasting yellow-green details at the center.
description: A stylized, translucent lavender flower with five broad petals and a yellow-green central cluster of stamens, featuring delicate veining and a glass-like finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open, overlapping lavender petals and the yellow-green central core.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the layered, curved petals and the protruding yellow-green stamen cluster.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the radial symmetry of the five petals and the clustered yellow-green center.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the petal curvature and the extended stamen structure.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, with five broad petals overlapping slightly at their bases and radiating symmetrically from the central core. The arrangement is whorled, creating a circular, layered appearance.
